title: Leelanau School
address: 1 Old Homestead Rd, Glen Arbor, MI 49636, United States

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Rich Milock","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWth-R3_07KuI6f9qD5ql0-zIT_0TKF7-zAbcYaqeZP81fCsLb5=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I really don't have the words to describe how incredible The Leelanau School is.  In the two years that our son has been attending we have seen him absolutely flourish, both academically and socially.  His feelings of isolation have been melted away by the compassion of the faculty and staff and, perhaps more importantly, by the love and friendship of a student body which is not only encouraged but _expected_ to treat all of its members with kindness and respect.  Academically he has been introduced to the kinds of concepts and ways of thinking that will prepare him not only for further education, but for a full life as a creative, productive, and engaged human being.  In this process he has consistently been challenged, been pushed to improve himself and to understand his own potential, but never has he been asked to do something beyond his means, been put down for having difficulty, or been expected to attempt anything new without the support and encouragement of his teachers and classmates.  My wife and I always say that if we had unlimited resources to create a school for our son, to handpick the teachers and administrators and to design our own curriculum, the result would end up being The Leelanau School.  It has quite simply changed all of our lives.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"John Neely","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUuwSLxgSlsa-tRyNeau0JyDvFhFWsJ7HyuySzyTth9ma_RMUqG=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Gear toward gifted under achieving students. They help students find whys to learn that will help them to be successful in school and life. Leelanau was an answer to our prayers, they saved our son. He went form not caring and struggling in school to graduating with honors, with several college admittances and academic scholarships offers to choose from.

Leelanau School offers caring teachers and staff that really want to change their student lives. The growth is our son was remarkable!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"christdragon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX7akwLj6KwFovRXsQ9W1XkTo3IAXfHEGXmKf0pmhBE8uIbOXWg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I hung out here when it was still a christian school. My grandmother helped take care of the headmasters kids on Faculty row. Lots of great memories.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alan Hejl","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUYdCqNoJd4hCtyhQ4mLdNCo2gUDkoB0mpv6cYPzf2I6KyV2iohlg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Bathrooms completely not wheelchair accessible here. ADA code for barrier free and standard sized bathroom stalls was established in 1990, and iterated again in 2010, and my wife was told that they were "grandfathered in", which is not a clause of the Federal ADA code or the State of Michigan ICC 117.1 2009 building code. I hope you don't have elderly relatives, slip on ice and break something, or ever have to use  a wheelchair or crutches if you expect to feel welcome here.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Owen Shapiro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKFqdn-t0QfU_IT9-4GKAE3FTkIXtVWDq9HDi0JRAjWgYp-5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The teachers are very skilled and the administrators are engaged and talented.   Despite its small size, it offers a good range of extracurricular activities, including arts, music, theater and outings.  They offer a very solid education in the core high school skills: math, literacy, science, etc.

It takes advantage  of its truly beautiful and unique setting in the middle of a National Lake Shore for teaching opportunities (such as for biology) and socially (for camping and outings).","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Christian McDaniel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhVd7hX5idBeBbFwcJ5vPLDlyiTq1WPgswK5A6FtDP1BclEA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our daughter is a two year senior.  This school has allowed her to embrace her strengths and expand her learning in numerous ways.  She is growing, maturing, and thriving.  We all love her school.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dean Dauphinais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXE6NLL0ZggK_eybyi9KGNkPOeQnF8e-y1JEm2KG6hPnHO82nlJgw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our son suffers from ADHD and spent 9th and 10th grades at our local public high school. While this high school is consistently rated as one of the best high schools in the United States, it was not a good fit for our son. Even with an Individualized Education Program (IEP) in place, school was pretty much a disaster for him.

The public school would give him "accommodations": extra time to take tests, the ability to turn homework in late, etc. But our son was still being taught in mainstream classrooms, with 30+ kids. He did not get the individualized attention he needed. As our son so eloquently put it one day, "What difference does it make if I get extra time to take tests if I don't know what I'm doing?"

Enter the Leelanau School in Glen Arbor, Michigan. Or, as we now call it, "The best high school on the planet." Just before our son's junior year began, we went to visit the school. The Leelanau School is a private, non-profit, strength-based experiential boarding high school school for intelligent boys and girls who simply learn differently. They specialize in teaching kids with ADD, ADHD, dyslexia, and similar learning differences. Their strengths lie in their dedicated faculty and small, supportive boarding school environment. Their mission? "To support the discovery of individual potential through academic, physical, and personal development of students who learn differently."

While driving home after our tour of the school that summer, our son said something very profound to me and my wife: "I think this school could change my life."

And change his life it has. Our son has gone from feeling "lost" in school to feeling welcome. From getting poor grades to getting excellent grades. And from not wanting to go to school to absolutely loving it. In just a few months, he blossomed into an excellent student, made lifelong friendships with fellow students from around the world, and did things he never would've done in public school (e.g., performed in a play and performed music and sang in front of a crowd). He has done these things because he feels comfortable in his environment; an environment where all the students like and support each other and where teachers give the individualized attention students like our son need to learn.

My wife and I honestly believe that if it wasn't for the Leelanau School, our son would not have received the high school education he deserved and was capable of

I highly recommend the Leelanau School without reservation. It is an amazing place.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Junren Wang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJRd4SagAR4QAv9XIEhi2Vxohqj6_8mS6Ky-yHByAyKz2pRug=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"","Images":null,"When":""}]

But other then that I loved this school they helped me alot .","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Micahh Thomas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUW69lfQvL7uzLMvTydZtG-H6ZiHN2SmxDaM9x7E6cXhbHVKlpC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"I can’t not seem to get through to anyone. The few people I have spoke to has told me to access my account for the enrollment checklist and it isn’t popping up at all on my screen. I need some help and I don’t have anyone to help me! I have been on hold for a hour. And have called both the general and the Michigan line.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lois Cooper","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJJjBh7iInj2f-OcMSc1rmiBPHvav-V_xU9mpYvkxYmJCB0Nw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"All the good reviews this school use to have is why I registered my daughters in this school to began with. But I hope others never have to experience what we have. I would give them a zero score if I could because they showed me that they don't care what the parents or students have to say. We had no problems for more then half of the school year, until the school counselor started stalking my kids. She would send them messages and put them in \"Bold Red Print\" And they would have no choice but to open them up. Most of the messages were about joining E-Sports. Some were not. Some of the messages were sent to me (the parent) and some of the messages were not sent to me at all!\n\"NOBODY\" in this school would listen to me and my daughters. My daughters were even threatened with suspension when we shared our concerns with the high school principle. And talking to other school officials did nothing except to make this lady's behavior more intense. Sometimes, this lady would send 5 messages a day. My daughters have been devastated by this whole thing. My one daughter had straight A's last semester. But that was before all this started. Now, my children are being ghosted by some of their teachers. I am not sure what her grades or my other daughters grades will look like now. But we don't care. We have never been through something like this before. My one daughter was so upset over all this that she even wrote a speech about what is going on at this school. And if anyone would like a copy of this speech I will gladly send you a copy. These people have access to your home \u0026 children. And in their most private moments too. I am just glad that I have a close relationship with my daughters and they tell me what is bothering them. The only thing this school has that is really good is a flexible schedule. But they won't let you go ahead...like they say...because the teachers will lock down the lessons so you can't go ahead very far anyways. And hopefully your child doesn't need extra help because these teachers are hardly available. My kids have been doing all their work alone all year long except in Geometry class. But that teacher is retiring after this year. All I know is that we will not be signing up for another year! We can't wait until this nightmare is over. I was going to wait until the school year ended to post a review but I wanted people to know about our experience. I know that parents are suppose to commit to their children's classes by the end of March. But maybe someone out there will believe us and might decide that this is not the right choice or environment for their child. All I know is that this was the most scary and creepy experience that we have ever been through. This lady happens to be a proud member of the LGBTQ+ community. And I don't have a problem with anyone in the LGBTQ+ community, we actually have family members who are LGBTQ+. But I do have a problem with people trying to force LGBTQ+ on my children especially when my children have never even spoken to this lady or asked for any of her services at any time during this whole school year!! Thank you for your time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gabriella Trejo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW599vPhkeVnNPWeHqNt0P6wSiuYGlav047TQSa-QbveTcPM2Id=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I admire this school because it absolutely helps me with my anxiety :3. My teachers understand what I've been going through, and I am doing good so far into the school year.","Images":null,"When":""}]

address: 2301 Plymouth Rd, Ann Arbor, MI 48105, United States
open_hours: {"Friday":["8:20 am–2:35 pm"],"Monday":["8:30 am–3:30 pm"],"Saturday":["Closed"],"Sunday":["Closed"],"Thursday":["8:30 am–3:30 pm"],"Tuesday":["8:30 am–3:30 pm"],"Wednesday":["8:30 am–3:30 pm"]}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Iman Ali","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKwvdcB1yblUpqp6IzLRjQk_gP8XDH_K84riKoEtl4BIduxBA36=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"MIA is the community’s gem. Excellent teachers, and excellent administration. The staff makes sure the children are always safe. Every year the school advances to match the fast pace of our life. Academically it is one of the top in Michigan. My children graduated from there are they were accepted in every university they have applied to with scholarships.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sarah Nafees","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqRttDExs6zykBiBqejULZ5AOzl6TJgPcMDhyEpCpoGvCXdD36=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I graduated from MIA a few years ago, and the enriching experience I had as a student inspired me to return this year as a teacher. MIA enabled me to develop the rigor, work ethic, and skills I needed to excel as a college student, and now as a professional. MIA also helped me cultivate a strong Muslim identity while sparking an interest in learning the Arabic language.\n\nThe school continues to grow exponentially in every aspect, from their excellent test scores, to the wide range of extracurricular activities, to the support services and resources available to students. The MIA community is like a second family, the passionate and dedicated staff here is unmatchable. In the dark times we live in today, MIA is a beacon of light for Muslim youth growing up in America.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Angham Hussein","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJJHc8kUj2IrQTW8LSqAozQ25X8UvK6r7UMhMLtLhbs33nXFg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's been over a decade since I've attended MIA, yet the experience has been truly unforgettable. The year I spent there has left such a lasting imprint on my life in every positive way possible. MIA provides students with a unique and rich educational experience.   You will never find a school principal who invests as much time and effort into the development of an institution. MIA and the city of Ann Arbor as whole, are very lucky to have an individual who is dedicated to serving the community by striving for the betterment of it's children. MIA will always have my love and support.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Samar Abbasi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLN5PDkybELyU6jANd6RlscXMn2NNmBAyzJph5MfnUD5BmJcA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Michigan Islamic Academy is a well-rounded school with excellent academics and dedicated, highly-qualified teachers who care tremendously for the students. Islamic principles are woven into the core curriculum and most impressive are the methods in which MIA employs to empower their students. Students are heavily involved in their education and are given the opportunity to take on leadership roles. For a school with a small population and small classroom sizes, the opportunities are endless. There are various clubs for students to choose from including NHS, SGO, MUN, Arabic Honors Society, Health Club, Yearbook, Robotics, School Improvement Club, and U of M’s Peer2Peer. If you’re looking for a holistic education for your child in a safe, Islamic, diverse environment, then look no further. MIA is a rare gem of a school.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Emadeddin Tanbour","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2oRUue56qlpvflVxENxFOiquB9lI7wl0m0-ZFgQMNfcAMQZIe=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"MIA is a great school and a place to send kids to be future leaders with well established positive character. Three of our kids attended MIA and are now attending U of Michigan and Case Western Reserve University. One of our kids graduated from MIA and now attending dental graduate school. MIA has expanded its facilities and added modern classrooms, science labs, and a brand new gym. What we like about the school is the dedication of the administration, staff and teachers and the strong multicultural environment. The school provides outstanding number of extracurricular activities ranging from student clubs, national honor societies and state wide competitions. Every student at MIA has ample opportunity to be active in multiple leadership and academic activities. The school has unique clubs that are hard to find in other schools due to the diversity of expertises of its staff, teachers and stakeholders.  MIA parents participate in tens of activities and wonderful gatherings throughout the year. MIA students practice public speaking, presentation skills in all media and are drilled through a series of communication skills practices that are paced across curriculum at all levels. Another unique attribute to MIA are the tens of activities and involvement opportunities that are provided to students beyond the border of the school. MIA students enjoy participation is out of town and some out of state field trips, conferences, symposia and the like. Good job MIA and we wish you continuous prosperity.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tesneem M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWgkdvyR6xBiO-kjjzyhJu_VwtWePvSSuZ5unzSBnabBjTkV8eG=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Even as an alumnus, MIA is like my second home. The teachers \u0026 staff are dedicated, qualified, motivated, and inspiring, and the environment is homey. It gave me a safe space to grow as a Muslimah and has honestly blessed me with a second (growing) family. Going to MIA kept me connected to my deen and to our Muslim community. And it has grown so much! MIA has bright, new teachers, a bigger building, and a gym. MIA also offers student support (counseling) now, led by the warmest women ever.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maria Tout","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI9rNTX1oVgjVihRLVJMhocDL9BvdOmyHazurFfA2Uaj7LloQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"As an MIA alumnus it is with confidence that I can attest to the value of the staff, education and community of the school. MIA helped me build an Islamic foundation that aids in my everyday life alongside unique academic and extracurricular educations. The teachers at MIA are what make the school the great institution it is and without their love and support I would not be where I am today Alhamdullilah.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sanjidah Rahman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXlaTbVk470NmM_nlJDSerAjMotrMtJAGiylC5m0Ppi4Omk6e68wA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love MIA! I started there at high school and I have been very happy since then. I made great friends and am being taught by inspirational teachers. I am so glad that I made the decision to attend there because it has improved my character by so much. Thank you \u003c3","Images":null,"When":""}]

On top of that, the school is run by one of the most calm, collected, kind-hearted people I've met; Mrs. Nhu Do. She is both incredibly supportive to every single student in the school and is a great problem solver in general. Even more, the WIHI community takes mental health seriously - something no school I have gone to in the past has done. There are always counselors available and the teachers themselves are very open to accommodating students' needs in order for them to succeed.
